text: City of Cedar Hill Page 1 of 4
The Planning and Zoning Commission of the City of Cedar Hill, Texas met on TUESDAY,
November 2, 2010 at 6:00 p.m. in the T.W. “Turk” Cannady/Cedar Hill Room, 285 Uptown
Present: Chairman Theresa Brooks, Vice-Chairman Bill Strother and Commissioners Gehrig
Saldaña, Lisa Thierry and Tim Hamilton.
I. Call the meeting to order
Chairman Brooks called the meeting to order at 6:02 p.m. declaring it an open meeting in
which a quorum was present and the meeting notice was duly posted.
II. Approve the minutes of the October 19, 2010 regular meeting
A motion was made by Commissioner Hamilton to approve the October 19, 2010 minutes, as
presented. The motion was seconded by Commissioner Saldana. The vote was as follows:
Ayes: 4 – Chairman Brooks and Commissioners Saldana, Hamilton and Thierry.
Chairman Brooks declared the motion carried.
and consider a request for a IV. Case No. 10-19 – Conduct a Public Hearing Conditional
within a Neighborhood Services Use Permit (CUP) to operate a Banquet Facility
zoning district, being 1.030-acres of land out of Abstract 233, more commonly known
Requested by Chris Conant of More Cabbage. as 1420 High Pointe Ln.
Chris Conant, 2484 Lakewood Dr., Grand Prairie, TX 75054, stepped forth to present this request
and answer any questions from the Commission.
City of Cedar Hill Page 2 of 4
Mr. Conant stated that he is currently using the upstairs portion of this building for his office
operations and that the downstairs area would be remodeled for the banquet facility.
Initially, he stated that they plan to host weekly “date night” parties for 5 couples, with a chef
Chairman Brooks opened the floor for anyone wishing to speak in favor of this request.
1. Joe Potter, President of the Cedar Hill Historical Society, 815 Knoll Manor Ct.,
Mr. Potter stated that he is in support of this request.
Chairman Brooks closed that portion of the public hearing and opened the floor for anyone
wishing to speak in opposition to this request.
Chairman Brooks closed the public hearing and opened the floor discussion by the
Commissioner Hamilton asked the applicant how many people he anticipates hosting at this
Mr. Conant stated that for the date night occasion he anticipates 5 couples or 10 people on
weeknights and upwards to 110 for an occasion such as a wedding, with seating both inside
Upon hearing of the possibility of outdoor activities, Commissioner Hamilton asked the
applicant if he was aware of the City’s noise ordinance.
Mr. Conant stated that he was aware of the noise ordinance and plans to comply fully. He
went on to state that they will not allow deejays or amplified music in conjunction with events
planned at their location, allowing instead only soft or string instrument music. He stated their
objective was to create a quiet, calm and very upscale atmosphere.
Chairman Brooks stated that an event with 110 guess could generate approximately 60 cars,
and asked the applicant if he believes they have adequate parking to support that many
Mr. Conant stated yes; with their parking lot and the availability of on-street parking on
Breseman St. and High Pointe Ln. Additionally, he stated that their site manager spoke with
officials with CHISD and that they would allow for the use of the elementary school parking lot
Commissioner Thierry asked the applicant if they plan to utilize the school parking lot on
City of Cedar Hill Page 3 of 4
Mr. Conant stated no, only on weekends and only if needed.
Commissioner Thierry asked the applicant if he ever anticipated hosting an event for up to 110
Mr. Conant stated that may be a possibility; however, he did not believe a large scale event,
such as a wedding, would occur on a weeknight.
Commissioner Thierry asked the applicant if the elementary school could utilize his parking lot if
Mr. Conant stated that the school district did not mention using their parking lot, but that he is
open to that idea. He stressed that they wanted to be sensitive to their neighbors.
Commissioner Thierry asked the applicant if he would adhere to the 10 pm closing time
limitation required in this zoning district.
Mr. Conant stated that in their contracts it states 9:45 pm is the “wind down” time and 10 pm is
the shut down time for events.
Referring to the applicant’s comment regarding a chef being onsite for certain events,
Chairman Brooks asked if there would be any cooking or food prep onsite.
Mr. Conant stated that there would be no actually cooking done onsite, everything would be
Commissioner Thierry asked the applicant if he was aware that health inspections may be
required if food was to be stored overnight.
Mr. Conant stated his was aware that health inspections may be required.
Chairman Brooks reminded the applicant that the Commission’s approval of this CUP request
pertained only to the operation of a banquet facility and does not give authorization for live
music. She went on to state that should noise become an issue with this use, the City can and
may take enforcement actions, up to and including revoking the CUP.
A motion was made by Vice-Chairman Strother to approve Case No. 10-19, as presented. The
motion was seconded by Commissioner Hamilton. The vote was as follows:
Ayes: 5 – Chairman Brooks, Vice-Chairman Strother and Commissioners Saldana, Hamilton
Chairman Brooks declared the motion carried.

This item was covered during the briefing session.
A motion was made, followed by a second for adjournment. The meeting adjourned at 6:20
